content.problemStatement:
**B**. A local library is digitizing its catalog system. They need a way to represent both physical books and e-books. A physical book is defined by its title and author. An e-book has a title, an author, and also a file size in megabytes (MB).

Your task is to implement this system using object-oriented programming. Create a base class `Book` and a derived class `EBook`. The `Book` class should have a `display()` method to print its details. The `EBook` class must inherit from `Book` and override the `display()` method to include the file size information.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single character, either 'B' for a physical book or 'E' for an e-book.
If the character is 'B', the next two lines will contain the title and the author of the book, respectively.
If the character is 'E', the next three lines will contain the title, the author, and the file size (as an integer), respectively.


Output Format

For a `Book`, the output should be in the format: `Title: [title], Author: [author]`
For an `EBook`, the output should be in the format: `Title: [title], Author: [author], File Size (MB): [size]`
constraints: Title and author strings contain at most 100 characters and may include spaces and punctuation.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains a single character: 'B' for a physical book or 'E' for an e‑book. If the character is 'B', the next two lines contain the title and the author respectively. If the character is 'E', the next three lines contain the title, the author, and the file size in megabytes (an integer).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line. For a physical book: `Title: [title], Author: [author]`. For an e‑book: `Title: [title], Author: [author], File Size (MB): [size]`.

sampleTestCases.input:
2
E
The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
Douglas Adams
1
B
1984
George Orwell
sampleTestCases.output: Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, Author: Douglas Adams, File Size (MB): 1\nTitle: 1984, Author: George Orwell
sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case is an e‑book, so the file size is printed. Second test case is a physical book, so only title and author are printed.

sampleTestCases.output: Title: To Kill a Mockingbird, Author: Harper Lee
sampleTestCases.explanation: A single physical book; output contains only title and author.

sampleTestCases.output: Title: Empty Book, Author: No Author, File Size (MB): 0
sampleTestCases.explanation: E‑book with file size zero; zero is printed as given.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.input:
2
E
Big Book
Big Author
1000000
B
Long Title With Many Words And Characters
Another Author
hidden.expectedOutput: Title: Big Book, Author: Big Author, File Size (MB): 1000000\nTitle: Long Title With Many Words And Characters, Author: Another Author

hidden.label: edge case
hidden.expectedOutput: Title: Zero Size, Author: No One, File Size (MB): 0

hidden.label: general case 1
hidden.expectedOutput: Title: Book One, Author: Author One\nTitle: Second Book, Author: Second Author, File Size (MB): 250\nTitle: Third, Author: Third Author, File Size (MB): 999999

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput: Title: Alpha, Author: Beta\nTitle: Gamma, Author: Delta

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each case read the type character. Store the title and author as strings. If the type is 'B', create a Book object (or simply keep the data) and call its display method which prints `Title: <title>, Author: <author>`. If the type is 'E', also read the integer size, create an EBook object that inherits from Book, override the display method to additionally print the file size, and call it. The algorithm is linear in the total number of lines, no extra data structures are needed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) besides the input strings – only a few variables are stored per test case.
